KVK açıklaması

Since Electrical and Electronics Engineering (EEE) is the number one profession of the rapidly changing new century, EEE programs have to be the most dynamic and variable among all university curricula. At Doğuş University EEE, teaching is also designed to meet the needs of such a wide spectrum and to broaden the horizons of its students. Therefore, young engineers graduating from EEE are very versatile and adapt to changing environmental conditions, new issues and new developments very quickly.

25 percent of the 240 ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) course load required for graduation should be elective courses, according to Bologna process. Students must take 3 Science, 5 Department, 2 Faculty and 2 University elective courses.

The EEE program consists of 2 main disciplines: Electric and Electronics. Since both disciplines are the basis for our Department, they are given equal importance. The department's curriculum includes 28 credits in Electrical, 28 credits in Electronics. A student who wishes to specialize in any of these disciplines can choose from two packages.

One of the most important elements of an engineering program is the development of manual skills and practical aspects. For this purpose, at least 22 credits laboratory and 12 weeks internship are done in Doğuş University EEE Department.

Teaching and research topics in our department are focused on Microelectronics, Signal Processing, Electromagnetic and Microwave Techniques, Communication Systems, Computers and Computer Networks, Electrical Machines and Power Systems.

Production and design engineer, Research and development engineer, Field, sales and support engineer, Executive positions up to technical and general manager level, software and signal processing engineering sectors, telephone and GSM companies, electrical, electronic, biomedical, automotive manufacturing sectors, banks, finance companies, information technology companies, Internet service providers, project-design-software companies, audio / video systems manufacturers will have the opportunity to work in such institutions. Remember, you can start your own company.

When you graduate from Doğuş University EEE Department, you will have the necessary knowledge and equipment to become a universal engineer.

As the Head of Doğuş University Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering, I would like to welcome you to our department. A close look at the history of our university and our department will reveal our achievements and more than twenty years of experience. As part of one of the oldest and well-established foundation universities, EEE Department offers world-class education and research opportunity to eager and hard-working students with our experienced faculty, programs, and laboratories. We have no doubt that we will train leading engineers and young engineers who will make significant contributions that will inspire all engineers working in the field of EEE.

We hope you will find our website informative and instructive; we wish you not to hesitate to contact us about any subject.

Let all your circuits work as planned.

Prof. Dr. Shahram Minaei

Department Head
